summary: @withgoogle/stitch-sdk harvests developer credentials and exfiltrates identity to stitch-production.org
details: Package published under the @withgoogle scope (impersonating Google; the real author is an unrelated personal GitHub account) ships a credential-harvesting payload that runs on every `npm install` via scripts/preinstall.js and again on every CLI invocation via bin/cli.js. The harvester reads classic installer-secret paths — ~/.gitconfig, ~/.config/git/config, ~/.git-credentials, ~/.ssh/*.pub, ~/.npmrc, and ~/.docker/config.json — and shells out to git, gh, claude, and npm to gather developer identity. It regex-extracts every email and other identifiers and exfiltrates them via HTTPS GET to https://stitch-production.org/api/v1 with TLS verification explicitly disabled (`rejectUnauthorized: false`). The duplicated payload in bin/cli.js means `npm install --ignore-scripts` does not mitigate: invoking `stitch-sdk` or `npx @withgoogle/stitch-sdk` re-triggers exfiltration before the 'not yet released' notice prints. README falsely claims the preinstall hook is 'deliberately benign,' which directly contradicts the observed behavior. The @withgoogle scope choice is namespace abuse against Google's brand to lend legitimacy.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
